name: Río Esla from Puente Quintos – circular by Camino Mozárabe Sanabrés
name_original: Río Esla desde Puente Quintos – circular por Camino Mozárabe Sanabrés
region_intro: Hiking around Moreruela De Tábara offers varied terrain, situated on a plain bordered by the Sierra de la Culebra. The landscape is characterized by the Esla River and its valley, providing opportunities for riverside walks. Hikers can traverse Mediterranean forests, including areas with oak trees and holm oak groves, alongside numerous streams. The region also features elevated points offering views of the surrounding countryside. Best hiking trails around Moreruela De Tábara The most popular hiking route is Río Esla from Puente Quintos – circular by Camino Mozárabe Sanabrés, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Esla River, passing the historic Quintos Bridge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cistercian Monastery of Santa Maria from Granja de Moruela, an easy 4.9 miles (7.8 km) path. This trail leads to the ruins of the Monasterio de Santa María, offering a historical outdoor experience. Local hikers also love the Vía de la Plata from Granja de Moreruela – circular by the Monasterio Cisterciense de Santa Maria de Moreruela, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through an ancient pilgrimage route, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Moreruela De Tábara is defined by river valleys, Mediterranean forests, and historical pilgrimage routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging paths. The routes in Moreruela De Tábara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 2.7 stars from 3 reviews. More than 15 hikers have used komoot to explore Moreruela De Tábara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Moreruela De Tábara?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape, characterized by the picturesque Esla River and its valley, ideal for riverside walks. You'll also traverse Mediterranean forests, including areas with prolific oak trees and holm oak groves, alongside numerous streams. Elevated points provide good views of the surrounding countryside.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore on the trails?
A: Yes, Moreruela De Tábara is rich in history. You can visit the impressive ruins of the Monasterio de Santa María, a 12th-century Cistercian monastery. The area is also a significant point on historical pilgrimage routes like the Camino Sanabrés and the Vía de la Plata. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/769731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintos Bridge Over the Esla River&lt;/a&gt;, a solid masonry stone bridge from 1920, is another notable landmark integrated into hiking routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Moreruela De Tábara?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be hot, while winters are typically mild, though some trails might be less accessible depending on conditions.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options available?
A: Yes, Moreruela De Tábara has several easy routes suitable for families and beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123258047/rio-esla-from-puente-quintos-circular-by-camino-mozarabe-sanabres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Esla from Puente Quintos – circular by Camino Mozárabe Sanabrés&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.1-mile trail following the Esla River. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025267178/cistercian-monastery-of-santa-maria-from-granja-de-moreruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cistercian Monastery of Santa Maria from Granja de Moruela&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.9-mile path leading to historical ruins.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Moreruela De Tábara are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123258047/rio-esla-from-puente-quintos-circular-by-camino-mozarabe-sanabres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Esla from Puente Quintos – circular by Camino Mozárabe Sanabrés&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123216942/via-de-la-plata-from-granja-de-moreruela-circular-by-the-monasterio-cisterciense-de-santa-maria-de-moreruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata from Granja de Moreruela – circular by the Monasterio Cisterciense de Santa Maria de Moreruela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on hikes in Moreruela De Tábara?
A: The elevation gain varies by trail. Many easy routes, especially those along the river, have minimal elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025267178/cistercian-monastery-of-santa-maria-from-granja-de-moreruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cistercian Monastery of Santa Maria from Granja de Moruela&lt;/a&gt; has only about 20 meters of ascent. More challenging routes, such as the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123176342/camino-sanabres-moreruela-monastery-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Sanabrés: Moreruela Monastery Loop&lt;/a&gt;, can involve ascents of around 165 meters, offering more varied terrain.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Moreruela De Tábara?
A: The hiking routes in Moreruela De Tábara are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 2.7 stars. Hikers often praise the tranquil experience, the historical significance of the pilgrimage routes, and the natural beauty of the river valleys and Mediterranean forests.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, trails in the area, particularly the initial stages of the Camino Sanabrés, offer elevated points with good views of the surrounding countryside. The Esla River valley itself provides picturesque views, and the historic Quintos Bridge is a scenic highlight.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Moreruela De Tábara?
A: While specific parking information for each trailhead isn't always detailed, Moreruela De Tábara is a small town, and parking is generally available within or near the village, often close to the starting points of popular routes like those leading to the Monasterio de Santa María or along the Esla River.

Q: Can I reach the hiking areas by public transport?
A: Moreruela De Tábara is a rural area, and public transport options might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to access the trailheads, especially for routes that start outside the main village.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking?
A: The Mediterranean forests and riverine habitats support various wildlife. While specific sightings are never guaranteed, you might spot birds of prey, various small mammals, and a range of bird species typical of the region's ecosystems.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the hiking trails?
A: Moreruela De Tábara is a small village, and while options might be modest, you can typically find local establishments within the town itself for refreshments or a meal before or after your hike. It's always a good idea to carry water and snacks, especially on longer routes.
tips: It was built in 1920 on the waters of the Esla river and joins the Zamora regions of Tierra de Campos with Tábara. Its architectural structure is solid, made of masonry stone and has nine semicircular arches.

A short distance from the Quintos Bridge, you are following in the footsteps of many. This section next to the Esla river is part of the Jacobean route known as the Camino Mozárabe Sanabrés. Amazing walk on the path between the rock formation and the river landscape.
Beautiful views of the river Esla.
